config_template.dtype: {DTYPE} (optional; accumulate in ttnn.float32 then ttnn.typecast back to input dtype for precision-sensitive paths; ttnn.int32 for integer mask/index cumsum)
config_template.reverse_order: {REVERSE_ORDER} (bool, default False; True implements the backward/reverse cumulative sum, equivalent to torch flip-cumsum-flip used in cumsum backward)
config_template.out: {OPTIONAL_PREALLOCATED_OUTPUT} (must match shape/dtype; e.g. ttnn.zeros_like(input, dtype={DTYPE}, layout=ttnn.Layout.TILE))

applicability_notes: constraints: {'layout': 'TILE_LAYOUT required for masked-index usage (callers insert ttnn.to_layout(mask, ttnn.TILE_LAYOUT) before cumsum)', 'padding': 'implicit tile padding must be neutral (tests call ttnn.fill_implicit_tile_padding before cumsum)', 'out_tensor': 'preallocated out tensor with mismatched dtype/shape raises RuntimeError'}; fusion_notes: Sequential cumsum over two dims (dim=1 then dim=2 on NHWC, batch=1) implements an integral image and can be replaced by the single fused op ttnn.experimental.intimg(features_nhwc). Reverse cumsum via reverse_order=True replaces torch flip/cumsum/flip chains in backward passes. Common downstream chain: cumsum(mask,dim=1,int32) -> add(past_len) -> mul(mask) -> add(padding_idx) -> typecast(uint32) for position-id generation.

placement_observations.source: test_examples: ttnn_integral_image_cumsum_channel_last deallocates input and calls ttnn.move(tmp) on the intermediate cumsum output to defragment/compact L1 between the two chained cumsum calls
call_sites: mask converted to TILE_LAYOUT and typecast to int32 before ttnn.cumsum(mask, dim=1, dtype=ttnn.int32); default interleaved memory config (no explicit override at call site)
